aiAnalysis.summary: A Hackathon PPT requires 5 hours of focused effort, which is highly achievable within the 24-hour deadline by structuring content creation and design across two distinct phases.
aiAnalysis.riskReasoning: With 5 hours of work required and 24 hours available until the deadline, there is a substantial buffer, making completion highly probable even with standard daily commitments.

tasks: Define the core problem, solution, and value proposition of your hackathon project.
Create a detailed slide outline (e.g., Problem, Solution, Demo, Team, Future Work).
Gather all necessary screenshots, data points, and key visuals.
Draft initial content for the first 3-4 critical slides (Problem, Solution overview).
dayWisePlan.milestone: Presentation outline and core content drafted.

tasks: Complete remaining slide content, focusing on conciseness and impact.
Design slides for visual appeal, ensuring consistency and readability.
Integrate any live demo elements or video clips.
Practice the full presentation aloud at least twice to refine timing and delivery.
Perform a final review for typos, consistency, and flow.

aiAnalysis.nextRecommendedAction: Immediately begin by defining the core message and structuring the presentation outline.
productivitySuggestions: Utilize a timer (e.g., Pomodoro technique) to maintain focused work blocks during content creation and design.
Prioritize clarity and impact over excessive detail; hackathon presentations demand brevity and a strong narrative.
Practice your presentation aloud multiple times to ensure smooth transitions and adherence to time limits.
